entities.name: FINGER MILLET
entities.description: A cereal crop whose wild relatives are used for genetic improvement.

entities.name: ELEUSINE AFRICANA
entities.description: Wild relative of finger millet used for exploring genetic potential.

entities.name: GENETIC RESOURCES
entities.description: The genetic material of a species, including wild relatives, used for breeding.

entities.name: CLIMATE-RESILIENT TRAITS
entities.description: Characteristics that allow a crop to withstand adverse climate conditions.

entities.name: NUTRITIONAL TRAITS
entities.description: Characteristics related to the nutritional value of the crop.

entities.name: SEED DORMANCY
entities.description: A state where seeds do not germinate even under favorable conditions.

entities.name: CONSERVATION
entities.description: The preservation of genetic resources for future use.

entities.name: EVALUATION
entities.description: The process of assessing the traits of genetic accessions.

entities.name: DISTRIBUTION
entities.description: The process of sharing genetic resources among researchers and breeders.

entities.name: PRE-BREEDING
entities.description: Activities to incorporate wild traits into cultivated varieties.

entities.name: THIOUREA
entities.description: A chemical treatment used to break seed dormancy and enhance germination.

entities.name: POTASSIUM NITRATE
entities.description: A chemical treatment (KNO3) used to break seed dormancy.

entities.name: GIBBERELLIC ACID
entities.description: A plant hormone (GA3) used to induce germination.

entities.name: ETHREL
entities.description: A chemical treatment tested for breaking seed dormancy.

entities.name: COLD STRATIFICATION
entities.description: A treatment involving cold and moist conditions to break dormancy.

entities.name: WATER IMMERSION
entities.description: A treatment involving soaking seeds in water to break dormancy.

entities.name: GERMINATION
entities.description: The process by which an organism grows from a seed.
